channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Sweden set to ban mobile phones in schools
item.description: Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.
item.content:encoded:
Long championed as a leader in adopting digital technology, Sweden is set to ban mobile phones in schools beginning in the fall for the next academic year.

(Image credit: James Brooks)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Federal judge strikes down Trump's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas
item.description: A federal judge on Monday struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The administration announced the fee as a way of preventing foreign workers from taking American jobs.
item.content:encoded:
A federal judge on Monday struck down the Trump administration's $100,000 fee on new H-1B visas. The administration announced the fee as a way of preventing foreign workers from taking American jobs.

(Image credit: Wilfredo Lee)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: More cases of screwworm detected in Texas, raising concerns for the cattle industry
item.description: A fly's larvae parasite that was eradicated from the U.S. in the 1960s has resurfaced In South Texas, posing a serious threat to livestock production. We report from a livestock inspection checkpoint.
item.content:encoded: A fly's larvae parasite that was eradicated from the U.S. in the 1960s has resurfaced In South Texas, posing a serious threat to livestock production. We report from a livestock inspection checkpoint.
item.dc:creator: David Martin Davies

item.title: The red state, blue state divide is real. But it's driven by more than just politics
item.description: Recent research suggests there's more going on with "ideological sorting" than simply moving to places that match one's politics. It's often one of many deciding factors, such as taxes or safety.
item.content:encoded: Recent research suggests there's more going on with "ideological sorting" than simply moving to places that match one's politics. It's often one of many deciding factors, such as taxes or safety.
item.dc:creator: Scott Neuman

item.title: Former Kennedy Center curator talks about the venue's future
item.description: NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Josef Palermo, an artist and curator, about his tenure at the Kennedy Center and what its future might hold.
item.content:encoded:
NPR's Michel Martin speaks with Josef Palermo, an artist and curator, about his tenure at the Kennedy Center and what its future might hold.

(Image credit: Aaron Schwartz)
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: Over 2,000 women attend Turning Point USA's annual women's leadership summit
item.description: Turning Point USA held their annual women's leadership summit over the weekend. Did the summit reveal a fracturing of the movement that helped elect President Trump in 2024?
item.content:encoded: Turning Point USA held their annual women's leadership summit over the weekend. Did the summit reveal a fracturing of the movement that helped elect President Trump in 2024?
item.dc:creator: Michel Martin

item.title: The return-to-work Survival Guide for new parents
item.description: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.content:encoded: Heading back to work after parental leave? Life Kit has some advice on navigating schedules, managing expectations and finding support during a major life transition.
item.dc:creator: Andee Tagle

item.title: Virginia's data center boom is raising West Virginia's electricity bills
item.description: West Virginia is all-in on coal while neighboring Virginia is moving away from it. But the same utility serves both states, making it hard to lower bills for customers.
item.content:encoded: West Virginia is all-in on coal while neighboring Virginia is moving away from it. But the same utility serves both states, making it hard to lower bills for customers.
item.dc:creator: Roxy Todd

item.title: Nurse and PA associations sue the Trump administration over new student loan rules
item.description: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks American Academy of Physician Associates President Tom Pickard about a lawsuit seeking more generous caps on student loans for PA and nursing programs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Ayesha Rascoe asks American Academy of Physician Associates President Tom Pickard about a lawsuit seeking more generous caps on student loans for PA and nursing programs.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Benk

item.title: Police search for suspects in Ohio shooting that wounded 12 near a street festival
item.description: Police still had no suspects in custody Sunday after a weekend shooting near an Ohio street festival wounded 12 people and sent attendees scrambling for cover in a busy Toledo neighborhood.
item.content:encoded:
Police still had no suspects in custody Sunday after a weekend shooting near an Ohio street festival wounded 12 people and sent attendees scrambling for cover in a busy Toledo neighborhood.

(Image credit: Paul Sancya)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
